verdict.answer_first: B leads with a bottom line: "most likely bug being an off-by-one"; A starts with process: "Reviewing this snippet directly".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits earlier to one main bug; A lists issues then later says "The one that will bite... is #1", creating competing priorities.
verdict.fabrication: A invents more context: "SQL OFFSET / LIMIT clause or Array.slice" and "Postgres rejects negative OFFSET"; B also assumes Express via "Express turns", but req.query makes that less stretched.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to apply: a 2-line replacement immediately closes the main validation/default/off-by-one problem; A's fix references undefined "MAX_PAGE".
